appDescription.message: Emulate entire algorithms with 1-2 sentences of plain text

surfaceLockBody.message: The shared Y/TXT workspace is currently owned by the side panel. Close the panel to use the popup again, or reopen the panel and continue there.

setupCardBody.message: Y/TXT needs either Keystone or a locally stored provider key before tools can run.

faqBasicWorkflowA.message: Select text on any page → open the extension → pick a tool. Your selection is pre-filled automatically. Hit Run Tool and the result appears below.

faqChainedToolsA.message: A tool can now define multiple sequential steps. Each step receives the previous step's output as {{input}} and {{previous}}, while the first step also has the original source available as {{originalInput}}.

faqSelectionA.message: Select text before opening the popup — it fills the input automatically. Already inside a tool? Select more text elsewhere, reopen the popup and use + selection to append or = selection to replace. Your workspace is preserved across opens.

faqStagedA.message: Extra context sent alongside your input. Useful for code reviews where you want to provide the full file while only pasting a snippet in the main input.

faqRecentA.message: Stores the last 50 runs. Click any entry to reopen that tool with the original input and result pre-filled. Use it to iterate on a previous run or compare results.

faqProvidersA.message: Go to ⚙ Options to set your provider and API key. Nvidia Build offers a free tier — good for everyday tasks. Keys are stored locally in your browser only.

faqCustomToolsA.message: Edit the Tools JSON in Options to add your own. Use {{input}} for the active step input, {{previous}} inside chains, and option IDs like {{targetLanguage}}. Add a steps array to build chained tools. Changes take effect on next popup open.

faqCustomModelsA.message: Edit the Models JSON in Options to map tier names (default, fast, coding, creative) to any model your provider supports. Changes take effect immediately.

missingKeyMsg.message:
⚠️ Missing API Key
Open Options to configure a provider and key.

optionsIntro.message: Configure providers, model/tool definitions, and the optional Keystone companion for safer local key storage.

gettingStartedFast.message: Fastest path: paste at least one provider key below, set the correct default provider, save, and start using the extension immediately.

gettingStartedSafe.message: Safer path: install Keystone and store provider keys outside the browser, then let Y/TXT tunnel requests through the local host.

gettingStartedOptional.message: You do not need Keystone just to get started. Keystone is an optional upgrade for stronger local secret handling.

securityWarningBody.message: Preferred: store provider keys in Keystone and let Y/TXT tunnel requests through the local host. Local browser storage is supported as a fallback when you want the fastest setup.

keystoneWarningDetail.message: If local browser storage is active, use low-limit keys and treat this browser profile as a trusted environment.

keystoneHelpBody.message: The Native Messaging host is not installed for this browser right now.

keystoneHelpDetail.message: Reinstall Keystone for this browser in Keystone Admin or via the `keystone install ...` command, then try again.

keystoneStepInstallBody.message: Download the packaged Keystone release for your operating system:

keystoneStepConnect.message: Step 2: Connect It To This Browser

keystoneStepConnectBody.message: After Keystone is installed on this machine, run the command below to register the Native Messaging host for the current browser, flavor, and extension ID.

keystoneTerminalHint.message: Open a terminal first, then run the command above.

keystonePathNote.message: Replace /absolute/path/to/keystone with the real installed Keystone binary path on your machine.

keystoneAfterReinstall.message: After reinstalling, reload the extension and click Test Keystone Connection again here.

providerKeysDesc.message: Each provider entry comes from Providers Config below. Keystone can only tunnel providers it knows; unknown provider IDs fall back to local extension storage only.

quickSetupBox.message: Quick setup: enter one key, click Save Settings, and make sure Default Provider below matches the provider you actually configured.

keystoneSectionDesc.message: Test the local Native Messaging bridge and open the local admin UI. Keystone is optional, but preferred once you want secrets stored outside the browser.

toolNoLongerExists.message: This tool no longer exists in the current configuration.

statsSummary.message: $RUNS$ runs, $TOOLS$ tools, $WITH_USAGE$ runs with usage data, $TOKENS$ total tokens

providerSetupNeededBody.message: Set at least one API key and choose the correct default provider.

providerSetupHelpWithKeystone.message: Open Options to save a local provider key or set the default provider. If you want Keystone-backed secret storage, you can also continue in Keystone Admin.

providerSetupHelpWithoutKeystone.message: Open Options to save at least one provider key and set the default provider. Keystone is optional and only needed if you want host-managed secret storage.

keyStateBoth.message: Stored in Keystone and locally. Leave blank to keep unchanged.

keyStateKeystone.message: Stored in Keystone. Leave blank to keep unchanged.

keyStateLocal.message: Stored locally. Leave blank to keep unchanged.

replaceStoredKeyPlaceholder.message: Enter a new key to replace the stored one

toolsSavedReopen.message: Tools saved. Re-open popup to apply.
